mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when policy is clearly easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS has fallen for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% tightens liquidity, raises the equity risk premium, and depresses near-term S&P 500 returns because discount-rate pressure dominates earnings support.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average 5-day return after the threshold is not below the unconditional 5-day mean by at least 25 bps.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS signals labor-market deterioration that weakens expected consumption and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal does not outperform either UNRATE or PAYEMS alone in walk-forward tests, or if 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of periods when UNRATE rises and PAYEMS declines simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in RSAFS above its recent trend indicates improving nominal demand and stronger revenue growth, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 3-month moving average by 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not show positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the signal’s 3-year rolling Rank IC is below 0.02 for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates non-inflationary real activity, which supports margins and cyclicals and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising INDPRO and non-accelerating CPI does not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if triggered returns are not positive in at least 58% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, recession risk becomes salient and equity multiples compress, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes fail to show negative 5-day SPX returns on average, or if positive returns occur in more than 40% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST signals weaker residential demand and softer wealth effects, which later drags broad equity returns lower over the following quarter because housing leads the cycle.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if housing weak-spot episodes do not coincide with below-average 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal’s hit rate is below 52% over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is easing while policy remains restrictive, the real-rate burden peaks and equities rally; specifically, a lower CPIAUCSL growth rate combined with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation rate declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined restrictive-policy/disinflation regime does not outperform the unconditional 20-day return distribution, or if triggered 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 57% of cases.
